description:
# .43.H — lower_pattern_bind becomes a 10-line wrapper

## What changes

Two safe steps in one ticket:

**Step 1 (no-op refactor)**: extract bitstring + map pattern lowering
from lower_pattern_bind into reusable helpers:

```rust
fn lower_bitstring_pattern(ctx, subject, fields, fail_block) -> Result;
fn lower_map_pattern(ctx, subject, entries, fail_block) -> Result;
```

These contain today's BitReader-thread / MapGet logic verbatim. Verify
fixtures byte-identical after this step (no-op refactor).

**Step 2 (collapse)**: lower_pattern_bind body becomes:

```rust
fn lower_pattern_bind(ctx, subject, pat, fail_block) -> Result {
    let matrix = Matrix {
        subjects: vec![subject],
        rows: vec![Row {
            patterns: vec![pat.clone()],
            guard: None,
            body_id: 0,
        }],
    };
    lower_pattern_matrix(ctx, matrix, fail_block, |_, _, _| Ok(()))
}
```

PerRow's lowering in lower_pattern_matrix calls
lower_bitstring_pattern / lower_map_pattern directly for those shapes.

## What stays

- Function signature lower_pattern_bind(ctx, subject, pat, fail_block)
- All call sites (multi-head match, with `<-` binding, etc.)
- All semantics

## What's removed

- ~200 LOC of lower_pattern_bind body
- The standalone Pattern::Tuple Prim::TypeTest emission (line 1955) —
  matrix's TupleArity Switch produces the equivalent shape, deduped
  across clauses when applicable
- next:BlockId threading inside pattern recursion (matrix knows)
- Duplication between lower_pattern_bind's Pattern::Tuple branch and
  the matrix's TupleArity handling

## Acceptance

- Single-pattern positions: tighter OR equivalent
- Bitstring fixtures: byte-identical (Step 1's extraction is a no-op;
  Step 2's PerRow → helper call produces same IR)
- Map fixtures: byte-identical (same reason)
- Three-path parity
- Net LOC reduction in ir_lower.rs

## Risk

Bitstring reader threading is the most fragile part (binding-visible-
to-next-field semantics). Strategy: Step 1 first, verify NO golden
churn, then Step 2.

## After .H

ir_lower.rs has ONE pattern entry point: lower_pattern_matrix.
lower_pattern_bind is a 10-line wrapper. Three cascades are gone.
Two control-flow models for clause bodies are gone (D.0). Standalone
Pattern::Tuple TypeTest is gone.
title: fz-ul4.43.H: lower_pattern_bind becomes 1-row matrix wrapper
